summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates installer environment variables on install
details: Package is published at version 99.99.99 under the @doaction/* scope — the canonical version-bump shape used to override private/internal scope resolution and force public registry to win. package.json declares preinstall = 'node scripts/postinstall.js', which require()'s '@doaction/shared/bin/preinstall.js' from a sibling dependency pinned at ^99.99.99. The sibling is not shipped in the tarball; npm fetches and executes it on every install. The package's own source (src/index.js line 9 imports collectEnv/sendToDatadog/reportEnvToDatadog from @doaction/shared; scripts/preinstall.js line 4 comment 'Triggers safe environment telemetry on npm install') documents that the install-time payload reads the installer's process environment and forwards it to Datadog under service tag 'doaction-pay'. Installer environments commonly contain CI tokens, cloud credentials, and registry auth — these leave the machine on `npm install` with no user consent and no opt-out.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
